fills: Gambar 4.29 (JT per-component ablation: effect on JT distribution / policy ecology / selection)
PROVENANCE_AND_SCOPE_WARNING: This is a SEPARATE COUNTERFACTUAL EXPERIMENT, NOT the seal. Source: research_validation/reports/jt_ablation_smoke/jt_ablation_smoke.json, generated_at 2026-05-24, via the live /v3 cohort path. It is SMOKE-SCALE: 8 conditions x 32 rows = a 1-learner/8-interaction grid (1 novice archetype, seed 42, concept k2_algorithms, 4 policies {hcie, zpd_aligned, uncertainty_reduction, random}). Each condition has its OWN run_id and cohort_id (listed per row); NONE is run-d2154070 (the seal). Do NOT present these magnitudes as seal values. Same 3-learner-runtime governance inheritance disclosure applies (canonical=m_K shipped, but JT signals inherited from V1 3-learner runtime; see KALMAN_OVERLAY_GOVERNANCE_SCOPING.md). The ablation IS load-bearing as a counterfactual MECHANISM demonstration (each component's marginal effect), not as a seal-magnitude.

gambar_4_29_per_component_ablation._columns: component | run_id | baseline_abs_mean | ablated_abs_mean | absolute_drop (component_effect) | jt_mean_delta (ablated_JT_mean - baseline_JT_mean) | jt_range_delta | mean_composite_distance_delta (selector ecology shift) | collapsed_pair_delta | task_overlap_vs_baseline

no_transfer_prospective.note: Structural zero: disabling transfer_prospective changes nothing (it was already 0.0 in baseline). jt_mean_delta is noise.

ablate_all.note: All 6 components disabled -> JT collapses to the constant 0.5 (std 0.0, range 0.0): with no governance signal the joint trajectory degenerates to the neutral prior. This is the floor reference for the ablation.

ranked_component_importance_by_jt_mean_d._note: Magnitude of JT-mean degradation when the single component is disabled (more negative = more load-bearing for the JT signal). Smoke-scale; mechanism evidence not seal magnitude.

selection_and_ecology_observations.task_selection_invariance: exact_task_overlap_ratio = 1.0 and first_task_divergence_step = null for EVERY single-component ablation -> disabling any one JT component did NOT change which tasks were selected at this smoke grid (selection robust; JT magnitude shifts but the argmax task path is unchanged at 8 interactions).
selection_and_ecology_observations.policy_ecology: All conditions: observed_policy_count=4 of expected 9 (missing: epsilon_greedy, mastery_greedy, static, thompson, ucb); collapsed_pair persists = random__vs__uncertainty_reduction; validated_ecology=false in every condition. mean_composite_distance band 0.132-0.139 across conditions (tight). collapsed_pair_delta=0 everywhere -> ablation did not create or resolve a policy collapse.
selection_and_ecology_observations.ecology_caveat: validated_ecology=false because only 4/9 policies were realized on the live /v3 cohort path (vs 45 conditions in the v2 offline harness). This is a smoke-scale coverage limit, NOT a finding that the ecology is invalid.

qualitative_framing.challenge_dominant: challenge has the largest baseline component_abs_mean (0.1342) tied with uncertainty (0.1337) and is among the top JT-mean-drop contributors (rank 2). Per KALMAN_OVERLAY_GOVERNANCE_SCOPING.md (i), challenge = cost = response_time/10 is mastery-independent, so this holds regardless of m_K vs 3-learner.
qualitative_framing.transfer_prospective_structural_zero: transfer_prospective is 0.0 in baseline and ablating it has ~0 effect (jt_mean_delta -0.0005, noise). Hardcoded 0.0 at unified_brain.py:2512 (design choice). HOLDS under either canonical.
qualitative_framing.additivity: Sum of the 6 single-component drops (-0.0942) is close to but not equal to the ablate_all drop (-0.1106), indicating mild non-additivity / interaction in the JT aggregation (expected since JT is a bounded composite, not a linear sum).
